concepts.title: Isothermal Process
concepts.conceptBody:
Isothermal Process

The process during which the temp. of the gaseous system remains constant during the entire process.

$\text{T}=\text{const}$

(i) Process equation $=\left( \text{PV}=\text{K} \right)$

(ii) Work done is an isothermal process

$\mathrm{W}=\int \mathrm{PdV}=\int \frac{\mathrm{K}}{\mathrm{V}} \mathrm{dV}=\mathrm{K}[\ln \mathrm{V}]_{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{i}}}^{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{f}}}$

$\therefore \quad \mathrm{W}=\mathrm{nRT} \log _{\mathrm{e}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{f}}}{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{i}}}\right)$

$\mathrm{W}=\mathrm{nRT} \ln \left(\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}}{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{f}}}\right)$ $\left\{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{i}}=\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{f}} \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{f}}=\mathrm{K}\right\}$

$\mathrm{W}=\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{i}} \ln \left(\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}}{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{f}}}\right)$

(iii) $\text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ U}=0$ $[\therefore \text{T}=$ const $]$

$\text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ Q}=\text{W}$

$\mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{K}$

$\mathrm{P}=\left(\frac{\mathrm{K}}{\mathrm{V}}\right) \quad \therefore \quad\left(\frac{\mathrm{dP}}{\mathrm{dV}}\right)=(-1) \mathrm{KV}^{-2}<\mathrm{O}$

$\therefore \quad\left(\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{P}}{\mathrm{dV}^2}\right)=2 \mathrm{KV}^{-3}>\mathrm{O}$

Comparison between two P-V plot for an isothermal process 

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & {{\text{P}}_{2}}>{{\text{P}}_{1}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ for }\!\!~\!\!\text{ V} \\ {} & ~\therefore {{\text{T}}_{2}}>{{\text{T}}_{1}} \\ \end{array}$

concepts.title: Isobaric Process
concepts.conceptBody:
Isobaric Process

The process during which the pressure of the gaseous system remains constant throughout the entire process.

$\text{P}=\text{ const}\text{. }$

slope $=\left(\frac{\mathrm{nR}}{\mathrm{P}}\right)$

$\therefore \quad$ slope $\alpha\left(\frac{\mathrm{n}}{\mathrm{P}}\right)$

concepts.title: Isochoric Process
concepts.conceptBody:
Isochoric Process

The process during which the volume of the gaseous system remains constant, throughout entire process, is called isochoric process.

$\text{V}=\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ const }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$

(i) $\text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ W}=\int \text{PdV}=0$

(ii) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ Q}=\text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ U}$ 
 

In the P-T graph $\left[ \therefore \text{PV}=\text{nRT} \right]$

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & ~\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ slope }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\propto \frac{1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ V}}\text{, }\!\!~\!\!\text{ slope }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\propto \text{n} \\ {} & \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Slope }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }=\left( \frac{\text{nR}}{\text{V}} \right)\left[ \therefore \text{PV}=\text{nRT} \right] \\ \end{array}$

slope of a $>$ slope of $b$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( {{\text{V}}_{2}}<{{\text{V}}_{1}} \right)$

concepts.title: Cyclic Process
concepts.conceptBody:
Cyclic Process 

The process during which the initial and the final states of the gaseous system are same, is called cyclic process.

$\to $ Cyclic process is represented as a closed loop on the P-V diagram.

$\begin{array}{l} {} & \left( {{\text{T}}_{\text{i}}}={{\text{T}}_{\text{f}}} \right) \\ {} & \left( {{\text{u}}_{\text{i}}}={{\text{u}}_{\text{f}}} \right) \\ {} & \Delta\text{ U}=\left( {{\text{u}}_{\text{f}}}-{{\text{u}}_{\text{i}}} \right)=0 \\ {} & \Delta{{\text{Q}}_{\text{cyclic }}}=\Delta{{\text{W}}_{\text{cyclic }}} \\ \end{array}$

Work done in a cyclic process

$\begin{array}{l} {} & =\text{ area }\text{ of }\text{ the }\text{ loop }\text{ in }\text{ the }\text{ P-V }\text{ diagram } \\ {} & \begin{matrix} \Delta\text{ W}>0,\text{ if }\text{ PV }\text{ loop }\text{ is }\text{ clockwise } \\ \Delta\text{ W}<0,\text{ if }\text{ PV }\text{ loop }\text{ is }\text{ counter }\text{ clockwise } \\ \end{matrix} \\ \end{array}$

Note : In the V-P diagram, signs of work is reversed.

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & \text{CW}\to \text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ W}<0 \\ {} & \text{CCW}\to \text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ W}>0 \\ \end{array}$

concepts.title: Adiabatic Process
concepts.conceptBody:
Adiabatic Process 

The process during which is no heat exchange between the system and the surrounding for the entire process is called adiabatic process.

$\text{dQ}=0$

[do not write it as $\text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ }\mathbf{Q}=0$, This is because in a process, the net heat change may be zero but in an adiabatic process, there is no heat change any step of the process]

Derivation of Process Equation 

$\mathrm{dQ}=0$

$\mathrm{dQ}=\mathrm{dU}+\mathrm{dW}$

$0=\mathrm{nC}_{\mathrm{V}} \mathrm{dT}+\mathrm{PdW}$

$\mathrm{n}\left(\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\gamma-1}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{T}+\frac{\mathrm{nRT}}{\mathrm{V}} \mathrm{dV}=0$

$-(\gamma-1) \int \frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{V}}=\int \frac{\mathrm{dT}}{\mathrm{T}}$

$\ln \mathrm{T}=-(\gamma-1) \ln \mathrm{V}+\ln \mathrm{K}$

$\ln \mathrm{T}+\ln \mathrm{V}^{(\gamma-1)}=\ln \mathrm{K}$

$\mathrm{TV}^{(\gamma-1)}=\mathrm{K}$

$\mathrm{PV}^\gamma=\mathrm{K}$

Process equation 

$\mathrm{PV}^\gamma=\mathrm{K}$ (1)

$\mathrm{TV}^{\gamma-1}=\mathrm{K}$ (2)

$\mathrm{P}^{1-\gamma} \mathrm{T}^\gamma=\mathrm{K}$ (3)

concepts.title: Polytropic Process
concepts.conceptBody:
Polytropic Process 

Any process is a polytropic process, if it can be expressed as

$\text{P}{{\text{V}}^{\text{x}}}=\text{ const}\text{. }\text{ where }\text{ x}=\text{ polytropic }\text{ constant}\text{. }$

$\text{ x}=\gamma$ for adiabatic process

$\text{x}=1$ for isothermal

$\text{x}=0$ for isobaric

$\text{x}=\infty $ for isochoric

concepts.title: Work Done in Polytropic Process
concepts.conceptBody:
Work Done in Polytropic Process

$\begin{matrix} \text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ W}=\mathop{\int }_{{{\text{V}}_{1}}}^{{{\text{V}}_{2}}}\text{PdV}=\text{K}\mathop{\int }_{{{\text{V}}_{1}}}^{{{\text{V}}_{2}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}^{-\gamma }}\text{dV} \\ \text{ }\!\!\Delta\!\!\text{ W}=\frac{{{\text{P}}_{2}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}_{2}}}{1-\text{x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ for }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( \text{x}\ne 1 \right) \\ \end{matrix}$

concepts.title: Molar Heat Capacity in Polytropic Process
concepts.conceptBody:
Molar Heat Capacity in a Polytropic Process

$\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} \text{dQ}=\text{dU}+\text{dW} \\ \text{nCdT}=\text{n}{{\text{C}}_{V}}dT+PdV \\ \text{nCdT}=\text{n}{{\text{C}}_{V}}dT+\frac{nRT}{V}dV \end{array}$

$\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{V}}+\frac{\mathrm{RT}}{\mathrm{V}} \frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{dT}}$

$\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{V}}+\frac{\mathrm{PdV}}{\mathrm{ndT}}$ (1)

This eqn. to be used when we have to obtain following : (in any general process)

molar heat capacity $\to $ process eqn. process eqn. $\to $ molar heat capacity

Now, $\text{P}{{\text{V}}^{\text{x}}}=\text{K}$

$\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{\text{V}}^{\text{x}}}\text{dP}+\left( \text{x}{{\text{V}}^{\text{x}-1}}\text{dV} \right)\text{P}=0 \\ \text{VdP}+\left( \text{xdV} \right)\text{P}=0 \\ \text{VdP}=-\text{x}PdV \\ \end{array}$

we have from gas eqn.

$\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} ~\left( \text{PdV}+\text{VdP}=\text{nRdT} \right) \\ \text{PdV}\left( 1-\text{x} \right)=\text{nRdT} \\ \Rightarrow  \frac{\text{PdV}}{\text{ndT}}=\left( \frac{\text{R}}{1-\text{x}} \right) \\ \end{array}$

Substitute in (1)

$\text{C}={{\text{C}}_{\text{V}}}+\frac{\text{R}}{1-\text{x}}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ In any polytropic process, molar heat capacity

$\text{C}={{\text{C}}_{\text{V}}}+\frac{\text{R}}{1-\text{x}}$
